lines.original: Gess how is back? (a d d y)
lines.translated: Guess who's back? (Addy)
lines.note: Transliteration: "Gess how is back? (a d d y)" — clear comeback announcement.

lines.original: Addy nagar in the house baby!
lines.translated: Addy Nagar's in the house, baby!
lines.note: Transliteration: "Addy nagar in the house baby!" — hype/self-intro retained.

lines.original: Kaley sheeshe gaadi ke
lines.translated: A car with black-tinted windows.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Kaley sheeshe gaadi ke" — 'kaley sheeshe' = black/tinted windows (status symbol).

lines.original: Baje woofer hai bhai (woofer hai bhai)
lines.translated: The woofer's blasting, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Baje woofer hai bhai (woofer hai bhai)" — loud car subwoofer emphasized.

lines.original: Level mat milawe
lines.translated: Don't try to match our level.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Level mat milawe" — warning to rivals not to compete.

lines.original: Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)
lines.translated: We're on another level, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)" — asserts superiority.

lines.original: Badmashi kare jo tera yaar
lines.translated: If your guy acts tough.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Badmashi kare jo tera yaar" — conditional threat/response if someone's friend misbehaves.

lines.original: Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)
lines.translated: The rest are just tutors, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)" — 'tutor' seems slang for lesser/unfinished people; kept literal conservative reading.

lines.original: Kale sheeshe gaadi ke baje woofer hai bhai
lines.translated: In the car with black-tinted windows, the woofer's blasting, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Kale sheeshe gaadi ke baje woofer hai bhai" — repetition reinforcing status and loud sound.

lines.original: (Woofer hai bhai)
lines.translated: The woofer's blasting, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "(Woofer hai bhai)" — emphatic repeat of the sound-system boast.

lines.original: Tu hai gentalman hum hai balak desi
lines.translated: You're the gentleman; we're desi boys.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Tu hai gentalman hum hai balak desi" — contrast between polished 'gentleman' and local/desi identity.

lines.original: Haan maine patayi teri chhori kaleshi
lines.translated: Yeah, I won over your girl, Kaleshi.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Haan maine patayi teri chhori kaleshi" — 'patayi' = wooed/won over; kept 'Kaleshi' as given.

lines.original: Hum hai neeraj chopra tu hai lionel messi
lines.translated: We're Neeraj Chopra; you're Lionel Messi.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Hum hai neeraj chopra tu hai lionel messi" — uses sports icons as a boast/contrast.

lines.original: Jisse bura lage vaaki aisi ki taisi
lines.translated: If anyone minds, to hell with them.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Jisse bura lage vaaki aisi ki taisi" — colloquial dismissal ('aisi ki taisi').

lines.original: Bachpan se star aur light se flashy
lines.translated: We've been stars and flashy with lights since childhood.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Bachpan se star aur light se flashy" — claiming long-standing swagger.

lines.original: Dikkat na hame koi aisi waisi
lines.translated: We don't have those kinds of problems.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Dikkat na hame koi aisi waisi" — unbothered, untouchable sentiment.

lines.original: Guru uparwala hum hai very blessy (aao yrr!)
lines.translated: God's got our back — we're very blessed, come on, bro!
lines.note: Transliteration: "Guru uparwala hum hai very blessy (aao yrr!)" — mixes reverence with slang 'blessy' and a call to friends.

lines.original: Full power londe andi no bakaiti (no)
lines.translated: Our boys roll in full power — no messing around.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Full power londe andi no bakaiti (no)" — 'no bakaiti' = no nonsense.

lines.original: Meri gang mein na londa koi chocolaty (yehh)
lines.translated: There isn't a soft guy in my gang.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Meri gang mein na londa koi chocolaty (yehh)" — 'chocolaty' = soft/weak in street slang.

lines.original: Thha ke lejau jaisе bukka reddy
lines.translated: I'll handle you hard, like Bukka Reddy.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Thha ke lejau jaisе bukka reddy" — threat invoking a tough figure; kept name as-is.

lines.original: Teri girlfriend bolеy mujhe hit me daddy
lines.translated: Your girlfriend tells me, 'hit me, daddy.'
lines.note: Transliteration: "Teri girlfriend bolеy mujhe hit me daddy" — sexual/playful bragging preserved verbatim.

lines.original: Mai ka dedu dose kare addy addy
lines.translated: I'll give you a dose — addy, addy.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Mai ka dedu dose kare addy addy" — left ambiguous 'dose' and kept chant intact.

lines.original: Arey problem bata de teri kar denge solve
lines.translated: Tell us your problem — we'll solve it.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Arey problem bata de teri kar denge solve" — offer of help/protection in plain terms.

lines.original: Maare aage pichhe duniya yo karti revolve
lines.translated: People revolve around us — the world turns for us.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Maare aage pichhe duniya yo karti revolve" — boast about influence and centrality.

lines.original: 22 saal ke hai londe lage desi arnold
lines.translated: We're 22 and look like desi Arnolds.
lines.note: Transliteration: 22 saal ke hai londe lage desi arnold

lines.original: Tere face jinna dolla height 6 feet tall (hanji)
lines.translated: You're roughly six feet tall.
lines.note: Transliteration: Tere face jinna dolla height 6 feet tall (hanji)

lines.original: Kaley sheeshe gaadi ke
lines.translated: A car with black-tinted windows.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Kaley sheeshe gaadi ke" — 'kaley sheeshe' = black/tinted windows (status symbol).

lines.original: Baje woofer hai bai (woofer hai bhai)
lines.translated: The woofer's thumping, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: Baje woofer hai bai (woofer hai bhai)

lines.original: Level mat milawe
lines.translated: Don't try to match our level.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Level mat milawe" — warning to rivals not to compete.

lines.original: Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)
lines.translated: We're on another level, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)" — asserts superiority.

lines.original: Badmashi kare jo tera yaar
lines.translated: If your guy acts tough.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Badmashi kare jo tera yaar" — conditional threat/response if someone's friend misbehaves.

lines.original: Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)
lines.translated: The rest are just tutors, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)" — 'tutor' seems slang for lesser/unfinished people; kept literal conservative reading.

lines.original: Bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai
lines.translated: Watch out — there's a Gujjar in the car, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai

lines.original: Arey thar fortuner’aa ke lagte nhi toll
lines.translated: Man, Thar and Fortuner — tolls don't apply to them.
lines.note: Transliteration: Arey thar fortuner’aa ke lagte nhi toll

lines.original: Gareeb se naa chhine roti dil anmol
lines.translated: Don't snatch a poor man's bread — the heart is priceless.
lines.note: Transliteration: Gareeb se naa chhine roti dil anmol

lines.original: Maare theke hai baarah (12) aur haiji kayi mall
lines.translated: We run twelve liquor shops and several Haji malls.
lines.note: Transliteration: Maare theke hai baarah (12) aur haiji kayi mall

lines.original: Aaja shopping kara doon pehle i love you bol
lines.translated: Come, I'll take you shopping — first say 'I love you.'
lines.note: Transliteration: Aaja shopping kara doon pehle i love you bol

lines.original: Main producer nhi dhundhta woh mujhe dhoondhte hai (ha ha)
lines.translated: I don't look for producers — they look for me. (ha ha)
lines.note: Transliteration: Main producer nhi dhundhta woh mujhe dhoondhte hai (ha ha)

lines.original: Aaj bhi hum ghaziabad khulla ghumte hai (yehh)
lines.translated: We still roam Ghaziabad out in the open. (yehh)
lines.note: Transliteration: Aaj bhi hum ghaziabad khulla ghumte hai (yehh)

lines.original: Hum unko nhi puchhte jo note chumte hai
lines.translated: We don't pay attention to people who kiss money.
lines.note: Transliteration: Hum unko nhi puchhte jo note chumte hai

lines.original: Nayi gaadiyon ki smell roj sungte hai
lines.translated: We get the smell of new cars every day.
lines.note: Transliteration: Nayi gaadiyon ki smell roj sungte hai

lines.original: Mathe tere jaiso ke yahan roj futte hai
lines.translated: People like you get their heads busted here every day.
lines.note: Transliteration: Mathe tere jaiso ke yahan roj futte hai

lines.original: Dil tere jaiso ke yahan roj tutte hai
lines.translated: Hearts like yours break here every day.
lines.note: Transliteration: Dil tere jaiso ke yahan roj tutte hai

lines.original: Ladkiyon ki tarah saale roj rutte hai
lines.translated: Those bastards sulk like girls every day.
lines.note: Transliteration: Ladkiyon ki tarah saale roj rutte hai

lines.original: Arey hatta saari problem ye sab joojhte hai
lines.translated: Besides that, these guys handle every kind of problem.
lines.note: Transliteration: Arey hatta saari problem ye sab joojhte hai

lines.original: Bete naare mere naam ke yahan roj gunjte hai
lines.translated: Young men shout my name here every day.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bete naare mere naam ke yahan roj gunjte hai

lines.original: Karte ho kaam kya ye saare puchhte hai
lines.translated: They ask, 'What work do you do?'
lines.note: Transliteration: Karte ho kaam kya ye saare puchhte hai

lines.original: Arey bhag bench*do hum toh bank lutte hai
lines.translated: Hey, run away — bench*do, we loot banks.
lines.note: Transliteration: Arey bhag bench*do hum toh bank lutte hai

lines.original: Watt! kine dega tu daam bata pehle puchhte hai
lines.translated: Hey! How much will you pay? Say the price — you asked first.
lines.transliteration: वट! कितने देगा तू दाम बता, पहले पूछते हैं
lines.note: Transliteration: 'watt kine dega tu daam bata pehle puchhte hai'. Taunting bargaining posture; kept literal-to-natural phrasing.

lines.original: Arey body main jaan hai aur gaam ke pradhan hai
lines.translated: I've got energy in my body and I'm the village chief.
lines.transliteration: अरे बॉडी में जान है और गाँव के प्रधान है
lines.note: Transliteration: 'arey body main jaan hai aur gaam ke pradhan hai'. 'gaam' = gaon/village, 'pradhan' = chief.

lines.original: Baapu police main aur tau kisan hai
lines.translated: My father is in the police and my uncle is a farmer.
lines.transliteration: बापू पुलिस में और ताऊ किसान है
lines.note: Transliteration: 'baapu police main aur tau kisan hai'. Conservatively literal to preserve family-status detail.

lines.original: Retire hai fauji aur lagta pathan hai
lines.translated: He's a retired soldier and he looks like a Pathan.
lines.transliteration: रिटायर्ड है फौजी और लगता पठान है
lines.note: Transliteration: 'retire hai fauji aur lagta pathan hai'. 'Pathan' used as a toughness/appearance stereotype.

lines.original: Dada ke haathon me ghar ki kaman hai
lines.translated: Grandfather holds the reins of the household.
lines.transliteration: दादा के हाथों में घर की कमान है
lines.note: Transliteration: 'dada ke haathon me ghar ki kaman hai'. Figurative 'reins' keeps the meaning of elder authority.

lines.original: 80 ka budha par lagta jawan hai
lines.translated: He's eighty but seems young.
lines.transliteration: 80 का बूढ़ा पर लगता जवान है
lines.note: Transliteration: '80 ka budha par lagta jawan hai'. Conservative, idiomatic English rendering.

lines.original: Bande jo mode to fail shah rukh khan hai
lines.translated: When he turns, the guy looks like Shah Rukh Khan.
lines.transliteration: बंदे जो मुड़े तो फेल शाहरुख खान है
lines.note: Transliteration: 'bande jo mode to fail shah rukh khan hai'. Kept as playful celebrity comparison.

lines.original: Bai chuley ki roti aur khaadi ki dhoti
lines.translated: He eats rotis cooked on the stove and wears a khadi dhoti.
lines.transliteration: भाई चूल्हे की रोटी और खादी की धोती
lines.note: Transliteration: 'bai chuley ki roti aur khaadi ki dhoti'. 'chulha' and 'khadi dhoti' kept as cultural markers for humble/traditional roots.

lines.original: Chain se khave bhai maar ke palati
lines.translated: He eats peacefully, brother, casually flipping it.
lines.transliteration: चैन से खाए भाई, मार के पलटी
lines.note: Transliteration: 'chain se khave bhai maar ke palati'. 'palati' = flips/turns the roti; preserved casual tone.

lines.original: Yaar sare takde aur mere jigari hai bhai (jigari hai bhai)
lines.translated: All my friends are solid — my close ones, brother.
lines.transliteration: यार सारे टकड़े और मेरे जिगरी हैं भाई (जिगरी हैं भाई)
lines.note: Transliteration: 'yaar sare takde aur mere jigari hai bhai'. 'jigari' retained concept as 'very close'.

lines.original: Jeeye khul ke jeevan me befikri hai bhai (befikri hai bhai)
lines.translated: We live openly and carefree, brother.
lines.transliteration: जीए खुल के, जीवन में बेफिक्रि है भाई (बेफिक्रि है भाई)
lines.note: Transliteration: 'jeeye khul ke jeevan me befikri hai bhai'. Keeps the celebratory 'befikri' tone.

lines.original: Koyi baitha canada me koyi italy hai bhai (italy hai bhai)
lines.translated: Some are in Canada, some in Italy, brother.
lines.transliteration: कोई बैठा कनाडा में कोई इटली है भाई (इटली है भाई)
lines.note: Transliteration: 'koyi baitha canada me koyi italy hai bhai'. Simple country-name listing as in original.

lines.original: Launde sare gail ke mere jigari hai bahi
lines.translated: All the boys from the lane are my close buddies, brother.
lines.transliteration: लौंडे सारे गली के मेरे जिगरी हैं भाई
lines.note: Transliteration: 'launde sare gail ke mere jigari hai bahi'. Keeps 'jigari' meaning 'very close'.

lines.original: Kaley sheshe gaadi ke
lines.translated: The car with black-tinted windows.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'kaley sheshe gaadi ke'. 'kaley sheshe' = black/tinted windows (status symbol).

lines.original: Baje woofer hai bai (woofer hai bhai)
lines.translated: The woofer's thumping, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: Baje woofer hai bai (woofer hai bhai)

lines.original: Level mat milawe
lines.translated: Don't try to match our level.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Level mat milawe" — warning to rivals not to compete.

lines.original: Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)
lines.translated: We're on another level, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)" — asserts superiority.

lines.original: Badmashi kare jo tera yaar
lines.translated: If your guy acts tough.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Badmashi kare jo tera yaar" — conditional threat/response if someone's friend misbehaves.

lines.original: Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)
lines.translated: The rest are just tutors, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: "Baaki tutor hai bhai (tutor hai bhai)" — 'tutor' seems slang for lesser/unfinished people; kept literal conservative reading.

lines.original: Bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai (gujjar hai bhai)
lines.translated: Watch out—there's a Gujjar sitting in the car, bro.
lines.transliteration: bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai gujjar hai bhai
lines.note: Transliteration: 'bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai'. 'Gujjar' invoked as a clan/community implying toughness.

lines.original: Bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai
lines.translated: Watch out — there's a Gujjar in the car, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bachke rahiye gaadi mein baithe gujjar hai bhai

lines.original: Level mat milawe hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)
lines.translated: Don't try to match our level; we're above you, bro.
lines.transliteration: level mat milawe hum tumse upar hai bhai upar hai bhai
lines.note: Transliteration: 'level mat milawe hum tumse upar hai bhai (upar hai bhai)'. Combined for flow and emphasis.

lines.original: Badmashi kare jo tera yaar baaki tutor hai bhai
lines.translated: If your friend acts up, the rest are just tutors, bro.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'badmashi kare jo tera yaar baaki tutor hai bhai'. Consistent rendering with prior 'tutor' lines.

lines.original: Bachke rahiye gaddi me baithe na rey fufa hi hai
lines.translated: Be careful; don't get in the car—there's a fufa (uncle) in it.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'bachke rahiye gaddi me baithe na rey fufa hi hai'. 'fufa' = paternal uncle; left literal with parenthetical gloss.
